Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents a single product available for sale in the e-commerce store.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

description.description: A detailed description of the product.

price.description: The current price of the product.

imageUrls.description: An array of URLs for product images, used for galleries.

categoryId.description: Reference to the Category this product belongs to. (Relationship: Category 1:N Product)

rating.description: The average rating of the product based on user reviews.

numReviews.description: The total number of reviews submitted for this product.

specifications.description: A list of product specifications as key-value pairs (e.g., color, size, material).

stockQuantity.description: The number of units of this product currently in stock.

brand.description: The brand name of the product.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the product was added to the catalog.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the product information was last updated.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Organizes products into logical groups for easier browsing.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

name.description: The name of the product category.

description.description: A brief description of the category.

imageUrl.description: URL of an image representing the category, often used for UI display.

Review.title: Review
Review.description: Represents a user-submitted review for a specific product.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Review entity.

productId.description: Reference to the Product being reviewed. (Relationship: Product 1:N Review)

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who submitted the review. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Review)

rating.description: The rating given by the user (e.g., 1 to 5 stars).

comment.description: The textual comment provided by the user.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the review was submitted.

UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Stores non-authentication related user data, linked to an external authentication system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity, corresponding to the external authentication UID.

defaultShippingAddressId.description: Reference to the user's default shipping Address. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Address)

addressIds.description: References to all Addresses associated with this user. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N Address)

orderIds.description: References to all Orders placed by this user. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N Order)

wishlistProductIds.description: References to Products added to the user's wishlist. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N Product)

Address.title: Address
Address.description: Stores a physical address used for shipping or billing.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Address entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile this address belongs to. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Address)

fullName.description: The full name of the recipient.

state.description: The state or province of the address.

zipCode.description: The postal or zip code of the address.

phoneNumber.description: A contact phone number for the address.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents a completed or pending purchase transaction by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who placed the order. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Order)

orderDate.description: Timestamp when the order was placed.

totalAmount.description: The total monetary amount of the order, including taxes and shipping.

status.description: The current status of the order (e.g., 'Pending', 'Processing', 'Shipped', 'Delivered', 'Cancelled').

shippingAddressId.description: Reference to the Address used for shipping this order. (Relationship: Address 1:N Order)

orderItemIds.description: References to all OrderItems included in this order. (Relationship: Order 1:N OrderItem)

paymentMethod.description: The method used for payment (e.g., 'Credit Card', 'UPI', 'Cash on Delivery').

transactionId.description: Optional identifier provided by the payment gateway.

estimatedDeliveryDate.description: Optional estimated date of delivery for the order.

trackingNumber.description: Optional tracking number for shipping the order.

OrderItem.title: OrderItem
OrderItem.description: Represents a specific product and its quantity within a larger order.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OrderItem entity.

orderId.description: Reference to the Order this item belongs to. (Relationship: Order 1:N OrderItem)

productId.description: Reference to the Product purchased. (Relationship: Product 1:N OrderItem)

quantity.description: The quantity of the product ordered.

priceAtPurchase.description: The price of the product at the time the order was placed.

subtotal.description: The calculated subtotal for this specific item (quantity * priceAtPurchase).

CartItem.title: CartItem
CartItem.description: Represents a product and its quantity currently in a user's shopping cart.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CartItem entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile whose cart this item is in. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N CartItem)

productId.description: Reference to the Product added to the cart. (Relationship: Product 1:N CartItem)

quantity.description: The quantity of the product in the cart.

addedAt.description: Timestamp when the item was added to the cart.

definition.description: Represents an administrative role. The mere existence of a document at this path signifies that the '{userId}' is an administrator. This is an example of an existence-based DBAC approach. No specific schema is applied as the content of the document is less important than its existence for authorization.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who possesses the admin role.

definition.description: Stores non-authentication related user data, directly linked to the Firebase Authentication UID. This structure ensures path-based ownership for authorization, where access is granted if `request.auth.uid == userId`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores a physical address belonging to a specific user. This subcollection structure enforces path-based ownership, securing `list` operations and ensuring authorization independence. Includes denormalized 'userId' in path for simple rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this address.

params.name: addressId
params.description: The unique identifier for the address document.

definition.description: Represents a product and its quantity in a user's shopping cart. This subcollection provides path-based ownership for secure cart management. Includes denormalized 'userId' in path for simple rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this cart item.

params.name: cartItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the cart item document.

definition.description: Represents a product saved to a user's wishlist. This subcollection provides path-based ownership for secure wishlist management. Includes denormalized 'userId' in path for simple rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this wishlist item.

params.name: wishlistItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the wishlist item document.

definition.description: Represents a completed or pending purchase transaction by a user. This subcollection provides path-based ownership for secure order history access. Includes denormalized 'userId' in path for simple rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who placed this order.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier for the order document.

definition.description: Represents a specific product and its quantity within a larger order. Nested under a user's order to maintain hierarchical ownership and authorization consistency. Inherits 'userId' from parent path for security.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent order.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent order.

params.name: orderItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the order item document.

definition.description: Organizes products into logical groups. This is a top-level collection with public read access, suitable for displaying product categories across the application. No specific user authorization required for read access.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique identifier for the product category.

definition.description: Represents a single product available for sale. This is a top-level collection with public read access, allowing any user to view product details. No specific user authorization required for read access.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier for the product.

definition.description: Represents a user-submitted review for a specific product. Nested under '/products' for easy querying of all reviews for a product. Includes denormalized 'userId' within the document for authorization independence for write operations (`request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId`).

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier of the product being reviewed.

params.name: reviewId
params.description: The unique identifier for the review document.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for CommerceStream prioritizes Authorization Independence and QAP (Query as Permission) compliance, critical for secure and scalable applications. All user-specific data is structured under a top-level `/users/{userId}` collection, making `request.auth.uid` checks straightforward and eliminating the need for `get()` calls for ownership verification. For example, a user's addresses are stored at `/users/{userId}/addresses/{addressId}`, where `{userId}` directly corresponds to the authenticated user's UID. This path-based ownership ensures that listing addresses for a user (e.g., `db.collection('users').doc(userId).collection('addresses').get()`) is inherently secure, as the Firestore rules will enforce that `userId` matches `request.auth.uid`. This design directly supports QAPs by ensuring that any list operation on user-owned subcollections implicitly includes the necessary authorization context within the path itself.

Publicly accessible data, such as `Products` and `Categories`, resides in top-level collections like `/products` and `/categories`. These collections have a homogeneous security posture (public read), simplifying rules and enabling efficient global listings without security concerns. Admin roles are modeled using an existence-based approach in `/adminRoles/{userId}`, allowing rules to quickly check for admin privileges without complex lookups.

For `Reviews`, which are associated with both a `Product` and a `UserProfile`, they are structured as a subcollection of `products` at `/products/{productId}/reviews/{reviewId}`. To maintain Authorization Independence for write operations, each `Review` document includes the `userId` of the reviewer. This denormalization allows rules to verify `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` for creating, updating, or deleting a review, preventing `get()` calls to the parent `UserProfile` document. This also facilitates listing all reviews for a specific product. This approach aligns with Mandate A (Authorization Independence via Denormalization) and Mandate B (Structural Segregation) by clearly separating public data from user-owned data, and ensuring that authorization logic is explicit and debuggable.
